title: Local Model Gateway
body: A Python service should call Ollama through a small gateway boundary instead of scattering direct HTTP calls across application code. The boundary owns model selection, request timeouts, fallback behavior, prompt assembly, and operational logging.

title: RAG Grounding
body: Grounded answers should include the documents used to assemble the prompt. Retrieval can start with lexical scoring for a dependency-free proof of concept, then move to Ollama embeddings with a vector index when semantic recall becomes important.

title: Model Lifecycle
body: Local model operations need explicit health checks, model discovery, request timeouts, and keep-alive controls. A production service should report whether Ollama is reachable and whether the requested chat and embedding models are installed.

title: Privacy Boundary
body: Running inference locally can reduce exposure to third-party model providers, but the application still needs prompt-injection checks, source authorization, audit logs, and redaction for sensitive fields before text is written to logs.
